commands.review_summary:
The PR changes OpenAI PI runtime-provider selection to resolve an eligible Codex auth profile before switching to `openai-codex`, and adds routing/e2e coverage for stale and valid Codex auth-order profiles.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main can choose `openai-codex` for explicit OpenAI PI runs from the first raw `auth.order.openai` Codex entry before eligibility is resolved. I did not run tests because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: No repair lane is appropriate because the remaining blocker is contributor-supplied real behavior proof, not a narrow code defect.

target.title: Fix stale Codex auth routing for OpenAI PI runs
target.body:
## Summary

- Stop PI OpenAI provider selection from switching to `openai-codex` based only on raw `auth.order.openai` contents.
- Resolve an eligible selected runtime auth profile before provider switching, preserving valid Codex OAuth routing while ignoring stale-only Codex entries and API-key-preferred OpenAI configs.
- Add focused runner coverage for valid Codex order, stale-only Codex order, stale-then-valid Codex order, and stored Codex backup profiles.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-codex-routing.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.e2e.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/auth-profiles/order.test.ts -t "does not discover OAuth profiles without inline credential material"`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenAI PI runs no longer route to provider `openai-codex` just because `auth.order.openai` starts with a stale `openai-codex:*` profile.

Real environment tested: Local source checkout on `codex/fix-openai-codex-auth-routing`, based on `upstream/main` at `91266fa928`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the focused OpenAI Codex routing unit test, full embedded PI runner e2e test file, the auth-profile eligibility regression test, whitespace check, and autoreview command listed above.

Evidence after fix: The embedded runner e2e test now covers the repro shape with `auth.order.openai: ["openai-codex:legacy-repro"]` and a fake OAuth profile whose credential material is empty; model resolution stays on provider `openai` and the embedded attempt model provider is `openai`.

Observed result after fix: Valid eligible Codex OAuth profiles still route PI OpenAI runs through `openai-codex`; stale-only Codex auth-order entries and stored Codex backup profiles do not.

What was not tested: A live OpenAI/Codex network call; the proof uses the existing embedded runner e2e harness with auth-store snapshots.
target.author: fuller-stack-dev
